strengths: Latency claim confirmed with large effect_size=7.06 (85.8% reduction), confidence=0.85
Experiment pipeline completed end-to-end with reproducible bundle
weaknesses: Approximation quality is inconclusive: relative L2 error=0.767 (verdict='inconclusive', confidence=0.7) — a speedup that destroys output quality is not publishable
Only evaluated at seq_len=512, head_dim=64 — this is trivially short for a paper claiming 'long context' relevance; no scaling results despite 'long_context_scaling' listed in evidence_plan_keys
No downstream task evaluation (perplexity, accuracy) — latency/memory alone cannot justify the quality–speed tradeoff
No p-values reported for any claim; memory claim confidence is only 0.65
Evidence plan lists 'ablation', 'falsification', 'predictions' keys but none of their results appear in the submission — incomplete experimental protocol
required_revisions: Run scaling experiments at seq_len ∈ {2048, 8192, 32768+} to substantiate 'long context' framing
Add downstream quality metrics (perplexity, task accuracy) showing the L2 error=0.767 is tolerable, or introduce gating/hybrid attention to bring error below 0.5
Complete the ablation and falsification studies listed in evidence_plan_keys
Report statistical significance (p-values or confidence intervals) for all three claims
Address the drop-in replacement concern explicitly: under what conditions is the approximation acceptable?
